finding: The boundary is correctly scoped to the t() function fallback chain only. No scope creep into adding missing keys or changing locale files.
The resolveKey function is a pure function with no dependency on Solid.js reactivity. The t() function wraps it with reactive access to dict() and locale(). Low coupling.

Missing keys that were previously returning undefined (and rendering nothing) will now show the raw key. This is a behavior change but strictly additive — it increases user-facing correctness by always showing something.
